intro: Apphud and Superwall overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Apphud is in-app subscription analytics, A/B testing, and paywall tooling for mobile apps. Superwall is remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps. Pick Apphud when the job is mobile, apps and you accept medium lock-in; pick Superwall when it is mobile, apps and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Apphud at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Superwall at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Apphud is a poor fit for subscription-only apps, and Superwall is a poor fit for subscription-only apps. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list. If your team already lives inside the ecosystem one of them assumes, that default usually wins — migration is cheap to start and expensive later.
quick_take: Apphud is for in-app subscription analytics; Superwall is for remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing; decide based on ecosystem fit.
verdict_a: Choose Apphud if a subscription at $0 / month and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to in-app subscription analytics, A/B testing, and paywall tooling for mobile apps.
verdict_b: Choose Superwall if some ecosystem coupling is acceptable and the work lines up with remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps.

faq.a: Apphud starts at $0 / month on a subscription model; Superwall starts at $10 on a subscription model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Apphud to Superwall?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Apphud carries medium lock-in and Superwall carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Both Apphud and Superwall rate the same on developer experience (5/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Superwall a good alternative to Apphud?
faq.a: Yes — Superwall is a reasonable alternative to Apphud for mobile, apps. The practical differences are subscription-vs-subscription billing and medium-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Superwall as a credible swap.
